standards_rationale.no-inline-code: N/A — hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is a Python hook script, not a command/agent .md file. Standard 1 applies only to .md files in .claude/commands/ and .claude/agents/.
standards_rationale.no-hardcoded-values: The /tmp/claude-* path patterns are intentional protocol state locations keyed by SID and nonce at runtime; they are not environment-specific hardcoded paths. CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var is used for the project directory. No new hardcoded paths or thresholds introduced in the diff.
standards_rationale.use-source-venv: N/A — the file is itself a Python hook script executed by the hook runner. It does not contain bare python3 invocations.
standards_rationale.integer-step-numbering: N/A — .py file; step numbering standard applies to .md files only.
standards_rationale.meaningful-naming: Filename pretool-git-privilege-guard.py is descriptive and matches no forbidden patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, *-v3, improved-*, better-*, new-*).
standards_rationale.english-only: The changed lines (141-142, 444-445, 454-455) are all English. The pre-existing Chinese text at line 11 is outside the diff hunks and classified as advisory_pre_existing per AC-2.6(b).
standards_rationale.merge-similar-scripts: No other git privilege guard hook exists in hooks/. This is a standalone specialized hook. No merge candidate identified.
standards_rationale.concise-documentation: N/A — this is a Python hook script, not a command/agent .md file. Standard 8 applies to .md files in .claude/commands/ and .claude/agents/.
standards_rationale.parameterized-scripts: All variable values (session IDs, grant paths, branch names) are derived from runtime data (PreToolUse payload, git commands, env vars). No hardcoded thresholds or identifiers introduced in the diff.
standards_rationale.dangling-references: All imports are stdlib: glob, json, os, re, subprocess, sys, datetime, pathlib. No dangling references.

notes.finding: Line 11 contains Chinese text in the module-level docstring. This is a verbatim quote of the historical user prompt that triggered the b5d447e incident. Standard 6 unconditional scan detected it.
notes.disposition: pre-existing — outside this cycle's diff hunk. The line is unchanged since at least commit aaf3c44 (refactor). AC-2.6(b) newness check: the changed-line ranges in this diff are lines 141-142, 444-445, 454-455. Line 11 does not fall within any diff hunk. Finding is therefore NOT attributable to this cycle and is NOT a new violation introduced by this PR.
notes.recommendation: Replace the inline Chinese quote with an English paraphrase and cite a ticket, e.g.: 'session 962de59f-fe0b-416e-b88b-7345fdf569e2, user requested a full commit-and-push sweep; see docs/dev/ticket-<id>.md for verbatim prompt'. Create the ticket file containing the verbatim Chinese text. Verbatim non-English user-binding quotes belong only in docs/dev/ticket-*.md per Standard 6. Adding task-id attribution alone to the code comment is insufficient — the comment itself must become English.

standard6_unconditional_scan.note: Standard 6 runs unconditionally against the full agents/commands directories per the exception clause, even in --changed-files mode.

additional_findings.finding: Chinese characters inside <USER_VERBATIM> tags: retry-phrase match table entries. File uses the documented <USER_VERBATIM> escape mechanism.
additional_findings.newness: pre-existing, outside this cycle's diff; NOT in changed-files scope

additional_findings.finding: Chinese characters inside <USER_VERBATIM> block under 'User's verbatim motivation (recorded for traceability)'. Wrapped in the project's own exemption marker.
additional_findings.newness: pre-existing, outside this cycle's diff; NOT in changed-files scope

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed: (1) Standard 6 finding on line 11 is real but pre-existing (outside diff hunks); reclassify as advisory_pre_existing per AC-2.6(b) — not a new cycle blocker. (2) The correct remediation is replacing the Chinese inline quote with an English paraphrase + ticket reference, not just adding task-id attribution. (3) The /tmp/claude-* paths are intentional protocol constants keyed by SID/nonce, not environment-specific hardcoded values — the no-hardcoded-values rationale should be worded more precisely. (4) No violations found in the actual changed lines.

findings.issue: Standard 6 finding at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:11 is a real baseline violation but is misclassified as a current-cycle blocker. Line 11 is pre-existing and outside the diff hunks (changed lines: 141-142, 444-445, 454-455).
findings.proposed_fix: Move from DRAFT VIOLATIONS to advisory_pre_existing: 'Line 11 contains pre-existing Chinese text in a module docstring. It violates the English-only baseline but is outside this cycle changed-line ranges, so it is not a new blocker for this diff.'
findings.rationale: AC-2.6(b) requires that a finding is new only when its line falls within the diff changed-line range. Line 11 does not. Reclassified as advisory_pre_existing.

findings.issue: The proposed rationale implied that adding task-id attribution to the code comment might be sufficient. Standard 6 requires verbatim non-English user-binding quotes to live in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only; code comments must remain English.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace line 11 Chinese text with English paraphrase and cite a ticket: 'session 962de59f-fe0b-416e-b88b-7345fdf569e2, user requested a full commit-and-push sweep; see docs/dev/ticket-<id>.md for verbatim prompt'. Create ticket with verbatim Chinese preserved.
findings.rationale: Recommendation updated in advisory_pre_existing notes entry to specify the English-paraphrase approach, not task-id-attribution-only.

findings.issue: The Standard 2/9 no-hardcoded-values rationale stated 'not hardcoded' for /tmp/claude-* paths, which is too strong. They are hardcoded protocol constants (intentionally so, keyed by SID/nonce), not parameterized values.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Hardcoded /tmp/claude-* paths are intentional hook protocol state locations keyed by SID/nonce; no new changed-line path or threshold was introduced, so no Standard 2/9 blocker for this cycle.'
findings.rationale: Rationale updated in standards_rationale for no-hardcoded-values to be more precise.

findings.issue: No additional violations found in the actual changed lines. The new docstring wording, _check_git_allowlist() call, and added diagnostic line are English and style-compliant.
findings.rationale: Confirms clean result for changed lines.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied Codex items 1-3: reclassified Standard 6 line-11 finding from violation to advisory_pre_existing with AC-2.6(b) rationale; updated recommendation to specify English-paraphrase approach (not task-id-attribution-only); refined Standard 2/9 rationale to accurately describe /tmp/claude-* as intentional protocol constants keyed by runtime SID.
